task_completion.description: Partition the 20m x 10m shell into at least five functional zones and name them.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view of Sweet Home 3D, there should be interior walls dividing the space into at least 5 distinct rooms. Each room should have a visible text label indicating its function (e.g., 'Service Bays', 'Waiting Area', 'Parts Storage', 'Office', 'Restroom').

task_completion.description: Place doors to separate the customer area from service bays and enclose the office, restroom, and parts room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for door icons/symbols on the interior walls in the 2D plan view, or 3D door models in the 3D view pane. They must connect the customer area to the service bays, and provide enclosed access to the office, restroom, and parts storage.

task_completion.description: Furnish the service bays and parts storage room with required industrial equipment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D view, the Service Bays area must contain at least 3 workbenches and 3 tool cabinets. The Parts Storage room must contain at least 4 shelving units.

task_completion.description: Furnish the waiting area, office, and restroom with required commercial/residential furniture.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D view, the Waiting Area must have at least 4 chairs, 1 table, and 1 lamp. The Office must have at least 1 desk, 1 chair, and 1 bookcase. The Restroom must have at least 1 toilet and 1 sink.

task_completion.description: Annotate at least three zones with custom text labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least three free-text annotations (e.g., 'BAY 1', 'BAY 2', 'PARTS') placed on the 2D floor plan. These are distinct from the automatic room name labels and usually appear as plain text added via the text tool.

task_completion.description: Save the completed floor plan as workshop_layout.sh3d.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar of the Sweet Home 3D application window should display 'workshop_layout.sh3d', or there should be a visible save dialog confirming the file is being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/workshop_layout.sh3d'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to create the layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open with active use of the furniture catalog, 2D plan pane, and 3D view pane, rather than terminal windows executing scripts to modify files.

integrity.description: The agent used genuine 3D models from the catalog for furnishings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Furniture items should appear as proper 3D models in the bottom 3D view pane and as standard top-down icons in the 2D plan, not just as hand-drawn 2D boxes or text mimicking furniture.

integrity.description: The layout was built inside the provided 20m x 10m rectangular shell.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exterior boundary of the building should remain a simple 20m x 10m rectangle as provided in the starter file, rather than a completely different pre-made architectural model.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates on a synthetically generated Sweet Home 3D workspace. The initial layout consists of an empty 20m x 10m rectangular shell (auto_repair_shop_starter.sh3d). The passing threshold is 60 out of 100 points.
